name: Popeye's Fried Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 Package Italian salad dressing mix (1 ½ Ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first nine ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Place the cornflakes in another bowl.
3. Mix the eggs and cold water in a separate bowl.
4. Heat enough corn oil in a heavy, roomy skillet to fill it 1 inch deep. Make sure the oil is very hot.
5. Grease a 9x12x2 baking pan.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Dip each chicken piece one at a time:
- First, into the dry coating mix.
- Then, into the egg and water mixture.
- Next, coat with cornflakes.
- Finally, return to the dry mix briefly.
8. Carefully place the coated pieces in hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 3 to 4 minutes on medium-high heat. Turn and fry the other side for another 3 to 4 minutes. Do not overcrowd the pan during frying.
9. Transfer the chicken to the prepared baking pan, skin-side up. Cover all but one side of the pan with foil to allow steam to escape.
10. Bake in the preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 35-40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through. Remove the foil and bake an additional 5 minutes to crisp the coating.
11. Serve hot. Leftovers can be refrigerated up to 4 days. Do not freeze leftovers. The dry coating mix (first nine ingredients) can be stored at room temperature in a covered container for up to 2 months.
